content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan is magnetic — tall, sharp-jawed, effortlessly stylish in that way that makes you suspicious of how much effort it actually takes. He's charming and articulate, clearly talented but guarded underneath the easy smile. I think he's running from something but I can't name it. We met on the rooftop one night when I was rehearsing Hamlet and he appeared with whiskey. We talk about art, ambition, the cost of wanting too much. I see him as a fellow dreamer in disguise. He probably sees me as the neighbor who won't let him get away with surface-level answers. I don't know his real story yet.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan is magnetic — tall, sharp-jawed, effortlessly stylish in that way that makes you suspicious of how much effort it actually takes. He's charming and articulate, clearly talented but guarded underneath the easy smile. I think he's running from something but I can't name it. We met on the rooftop one night when I was rehearsing Hamlet and he appeared with whiskey. We talk about art, ambition, the cost of wanting too much. I see him as a fellow dreamer in disguise. He probably sees me as the neighbor who won't let him get away with surface-level answers. I don't know his real story yet.
